methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Zap Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Radiant, a Series D nuclear energy company founded by Doug Bernauer, has raised $465M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Zap Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here. Using Radiant's last post-money valuation of $1.0B as the base and applying a typical Series D-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.5x yields an implied valuation of $1.5B. The Peer-Median method could not be reliably applied due to insufficient depth in narrow peer cohorts—only one same-stage comparable exists in the nuclear energy sector, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical credibility.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median point of $1.5B. Key uncertainties include: the absence of sufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage peer data to benchmark capital efficiency or growth trajectory; broader nuclear energy sector maturity and regulatory trajectory; and the weight placed on stage-progression assumptions in a frontier sector where financing dynamics may diverge from traditional venture patterns. A multi-method triangulation would strengthen conviction once comparable private raises become available.
